title: import-tidy
text: goal plate2N facilitate data wrangling data analysis raw data originates absorbance readings 96-well plate reader (spectrophotometry). particular vignette adresses first steps pipeline: getting files raw data various forms tidy data table can easily manipulated downstream steps. final tidy_table look something like : vignette, cover three steps: 1) Data import various raw file formats txt_to_tibble(), csv_to_tibble() skanit_to_tibble(), data brought shape common import file. refer output first step object called “tibble” data still “plate” format (see hereunder). tibble, first columns contains plate id first row, followed letters caracterising 8 rows plate (H). rest first row contains plate-column numbers (1 12). rest table contains original plate data, can numerical (e.g., absorbance data) characters (e.g., plate mapping) 2) Verticalization “tibble” verticalized table verticalize_plates(). vertical_plates present data verticalized format, .e., data plate displayed single column name column plate id. first 2 columns contain well identifier, stored columns “row” (plate, H), “column” (1 12). vertical format good format select subset plates (e.g., based experiment, treatment, anything else user stored column names). See example hereunder: 3) Tidying verticalized plate tidy_table displayed beginning vignette, format easily usable downstream data transformations analyses. step done using tidyr package Note: need arises fit package plate formats 96-well plates, please reach authors.

title: import-tidy
text: 3 import functions hereunder (csv_to_tibble(), skanit_to_tibble() txt_to_tibble()) can used import absorbance mapping data single tibble. However, case, recommended attribute different plate ids 2 “plates” represent plate (e.g., “NO3_plate1_abs” “NO3_plate1_map”, see also description verticalize_plates() section 2 see final output).

title: import-tidy
text: function csv_to_tibble() imports plate data csv file data structured specific way (see hereunder). csv formats, see sections “skanit csv” “formats”. required data structure strictly outputted tibble (see tibble_example hereabove). sense, function csv_to_tibble()constitutes simplest import option takes advantage readr package (part tidyverse), using either function read_csv() read_csv2(), comma-separated semi-colon separated values, respectively, can modulated parameter delim, defaults value "," (read_csv()), accepts ";" (read_csv2()). example call csv_to_tibble() function.

title: import-tidy
text: “skanit csv” csv data structured also plate format, plates organized differently, according format generated software called Skanit linked plate reader. raw csv format can observed raw data attached package displayed hereunder. make sense manually create csv file “skanit csv” format, using function skanit_to_tibble() useful users Skanit software. See also ?skanit_to_tibble() details. example call skanit_to_tibble() function see output. Notice identical structure skanit$abs_tibble skanit$map_tibble.

title: import-tidy
text: function txt_to_tibble() specific logic. Indeed, whereas csv_to_tibble() skanit_to_tibble() take single file input, txt_to_tibble() takes single folder input, indefinite amount single .TXT files can found. however important folder contains .TXT files input data, though may contain file types. details file requirements can found ?txt_to_tibble(). example folder containing 5 .TXT files. .TXT file contains absorbance data single plate. means , create tibble, txt_to_tibble() iterates .TXT files given folder (txt_folder example ), extracts plate data, adds plate id upper left corner plate data, combines plates single tibble. examine aspect raw file, go manually visit file path displayed hereunder txt_folder computer’s file directory. Alternatively, can see example raw .TXT file github repository: Add link something. Indeed, raw file structure easily shown import functions return error messages unless add additional tweaking extract plate data. error messages come column-structure .TXT file confusing import functions (see tentative ). Therefore import readr::read_tsv() already needs skip lines extracts plate data, shown , display raw file structure. Just skanit_to_tibble(), make little sense use txt_to_tibble() extract plate data .TXT files generated another raw structure one provided . Indeed, just “skanit csv”, “txt” structure automatically generated plate reader (Add reference plate reader) data exported skanit software, indeed directly exported txt. Note: raw .TXT files different structure, please reach , can propose adapted importing step function definition.

title: import-tidy
text: data different raw formats, 2 options. 1) small data sets, quick option simply copy-paste raw plate data clean csv sheet (outside R, example Excel), adopting raw structure tibble_example. can generate empty csv template using function Add name funtion. can lookup csv structure following file: 2) bigger data sets, recommend using export function readr package (e.g., readr::read_csv(), readr::read_tsv() readr::read_delim()), possibly selection columns rows, using tools dplyr tidyr packages bring resulting tibble required format.

title: import-tidy
text: practice, often import absorbance data one function (typically skanit_to_tibble() txt_to_tibble()) mapping data another (typically csv_to_tibble() even simply readr::read_csv()). means , end, need verticalize datasets, also need merge single data set. join_ab_map() just . works directly imported tibbles, uses verticalize_plates() followed dplyr::left_join() given series default presets match exactly needs adding prefix column names incorporate information dataset (optional) data type (absorbance vs mapping). look new joined table, recognize verticalized structures, now names added double prefix, containing “expe1” attributed argument dataset either “abs” “map”. Defining dataset column names makes sense especially several datasets joined line. “abs/map” prefixes can modulated adapting default value argument abs_map = c("abs-", "map") Additional info can found ?join_abs_map().

title: import-tidy
text: : change section reflect new function vertical_to_tidy() vertical format practical manual/human handling, easy--handle number rows (96), individual plates easy select based plate name. example, easy moment subset plates based dataset, abs/map even features embedded plate name (N species case plate names contained either “NO3”, “NO2” “NH4”). can easily done using dplyr package function dplyr::select(), aided helper functions tidyselect package, starts_with(), contains(), etc. verticalized format ideal bulk computing transformations, might tidyverse sits computations done columns. , typical last step tidying bring dataset format single column absorbance date, another mapping data, unique well represented row. tidyr “dplyr packages job wonderfully feel need create function, user might want adapt final tidy table need. However, show one example use successively tidyr::pivot_longer()tidyr::pivot_wider() get data need sending computational/analytical pipelines. First, use pivot longer get columns compacted way. end, need separate absorbance data (numerical) mapping data (strings), come next step went bit far. want, one column absorbance data, another one mapping. call pivot_wider() next chunk. next steps pipeline (dplyr::relocate() dplyr::mutate()) serve improve table, additional columns might prove useful later (well_id unique_well_id).
code: (too_long <- joined_vertical |>    tidyr::pivot_longer(     cols = !any_of(c("row", "column")),     names_to = c("dataset", "abs_map", "plate_id"),     names_sep = "-",     values_to = "value"     )) #> # A tibble: 1,920 × 6 #>    row   column dataset abs_map plate_id value  #>    <chr> <chr>  <chr>   <chr>   <chr>    <chr>  #>  1 A     1      expe1   abs     M12      1.4402 #>  2 A     1      expe1   abs     M16      1.5789 #>  3 A     1      expe1   abs     M17      1.5611 #>  4 A     1      expe1   abs     M18      1.7013 #>  5 A     1      expe1   abs     M19      1.6865 #>  6 A     1      expe1   abs     M20      1.7936 #>  7 A     1      expe1   abs     M21      1.7925 #>  8 A     1      expe1   abs     M22      1.8274 #>  9 A     1      expe1   abs     M23      1.9330 #> 10 A     1      expe1   abs     M1       0.9254 #> # ℹ 1,910 more rows (data_tidy <- too_long |>    tidyr::pivot_wider(     names_from = "abs_map",     values_from = "value"   ) |>    dplyr::relocate(map, .before = "abs" ) |>    dplyr::mutate(     well_id = paste0(row, column),     unique_well_id = paste0(well_id, "_", plate_id),     .before = 3   )) #> # A tibble: 960 × 8 #>    row   column well_id unique_well_id dataset plate_id map     abs    #>    <chr> <chr>  <chr>   <chr>          <chr>   <chr>    <chr>   <chr>  #>  1 A     1      A1      A1_M12         expe1   M12      Std0001 1.4402 #>  2 A     1      A1      A1_M16         expe1   M16      Std0097 1.5789 #>  3 A     1      A1      A1_M17         expe1   M17      Std0193 1.5611 #>  4 A     1      A1      A1_M18         expe1   M18      Std0289 1.7013 #>  5 A     1      A1      A1_M19         expe1   M19      Std0385 1.6865 #>  6 A     1      A1      A1_M20         expe1   M20      Std0481 1.7936 #>  7 A     1      A1      A1_M21         expe1   M21      Std0577 1.7925 #>  8 A     1      A1      A1_M22         expe1   M22      Std0673 1.8274 #>  9 A     1      A1      A1_M23         expe1   M23      Std0769 1.9330 #> 10 A     1      A1      A1_M1          expe1   M1       Std0865 0.9254 #> # what: 5 - Final note: issues when absorbance and mapping data with identical plate ids are imported from a single file.
title: import-tidy
text: Usually, physical plate used lab come 2 data tables plate format. Indeed, measure absorbance well needs accompanied mapping information (e.g., well corresponds also sample id information treatment). Consequently, single plate (let’s call “NO3_plate1”) result import least 2 “plate data tables”. 2 plate data sets characterizing plate need handled differently downstream analysis. see 2 typical use cases 1) 2 () plate data sets separated separate raw files imported (e.g., absorbance data raw .TXT mapping data recorded .csv). case, 2 distinct tibbles created, quite easy manipulate separately subsequent steps use join_abs_map() merge take profit automatic prefix function adds verticalization/merging process. 2) 2 () plate data sets imported raw file. can done several ways. data imported “skanit csv” mapping plates encoded. default, absorbance mapping data returned 2 separate items list generated skanit_to_csv() can easily manipulated separately, e.g., using join_abs_map() mentioned . data imported single csv file. particular case, recommended name 2 “plate data” distinct plate ids (e.g., “abs-NO3_plate1” “map-NO3_plate1”), simply considered distinct plates function csv_to_skanit(), can easily selected separately subsequent steps, example, taking advantage helper function tidyselect::starts_with() (try verticalized plate: dplyr::select(my_vertical_plate, tidyselect::starts_with("abs"))).

title: Identify outlier-wells within plates containing suspicious extractant data — boxplot_outlier_extr
code: data <- tidy_plates # 0.5 is unreasonable in most uses, but is used here to ensure some output suspicious_plate_id <- qc_raw_extr(    data, max_coeff = 0.5, suppress_message = TRUE, suppress_warning = TRUE) suspicious_extr <- suspicious_extr(    data, max_coeff = 0.5, suspicious_plate_id = suspicious_plate_id) boxplot_outlier_extr(     suspicious_extractant = suspicious_extr,     max_coeff = 0.5)

what: Replaces raw absorbance data from standard curves by blank-corrected absorbance values. — correct_std_blank
title: Replaces raw absorbance data from standard curves by blank-corrected absorbance values. — correct_std_blank
text: correct_std_blank() relies extract_std_blanc().

title: Replaces raw absorbance data from standard curves by blank-corrected absorbance values. — correct_std_blank
code: correct_std_blank(   data,   std_def = "Std",   pipetting_direction = "top_down",   std_blank_average = NULL,   std_blank_trusted = NULL,   std_blank = NULL )

title: Replaces raw absorbance data from standard curves by blank-corrected absorbance values. — correct_std_blank
text: data tibble respecting structure tidy_table. data must , though necessarily order, following column names: row, column, well_id, unique_well_id, dataset, plate_id, map, abs std_def string, defaults "Std": data wells containing standard curve referred . pipetting_direction Can "top_down" (default) "bottom_up". top_down pipetting means curve pipetted vertically (single column 96-well plate), smallest value (blank) row highest value row H. Conversely, bottom_up pipetting blank row H concentrated solution row std_blank_average NULL (default), computed std_blank_trusted. Otherwise, std_blank_average tibble format extract_std_blanc(data)$average Changing default value std_blanc_average may relevant previous call extract_std_blanc() led user correct "trusted" blancs way (see ?extract_std_blanc() details) std_blank_trusted NULL (default), extracted std_blank std_blank NULL (default), extracted/computed data, using extract_std_blanc().

title: Replaces raw absorbance data from standard curves by blank-corrected absorbance values. — correct_std_blank
text: tibble blank-corrected absorbance values standard curves. less rows input data - correct_std_blank() extracts standard curves-related data - keeps values non-blank wells correction done, row (top_down pipetting) row H (bottom_up pipetting) missing output table

title: Replaces raw absorbance data from standard curves by blank-corrected absorbance values. — correct_std_blank

what: Import 96-well plate data in csv format — csv_to_tibble
title: Import 96-well plate data in csv format — csv_to_tibble
text: Can either comma-delimited ("normal csv" = default setting) semi-colon delimited ("French version" commas used digits semi-colons separators). format must follow strictly following structure, content can numerical (e.g., absorbance data) strings (e.g., plate mapping): plates must directly top (empty rows plates) first plate must top document (empty row ) plates must utmost left document (first 13 columns sheet) data must recorded beyond plate data (nothing plates right) plate name must first cell (top left) plate, just "", marking first row data, just left "1", marking first column data csv files fit neither structure "Skanit" structure (see ?skanit_to_plate details), alternative use read_csv() read_csv2 rearrange resulting tibble fit output given , can inputted downstream steps.

what: Extraction and Quality Check (QC) of blank values for standard curves — extract_std_blanc
title: Extraction and Quality Check (QC) of blank values for standard curves — extract_std_blanc
text: cases blank standard curve blank samples, separate blank correction must happen subsets data. , standard curve one well containing blank value, usually pipetted row (pipetting_direction = "top_down"), row H (pipetting_direction = "bottom_up").

title: Extraction and Quality Check (QC) of blank values for standard curves — extract_std_blanc
Extraction and Quality Check (QC) of blank values for standard curves — extract_std_blanc
text: data tibble respecting structure tidy_table. data must , though necessarily order, following column names: row, column, well_id, unique_well_id, dataset, plate_id, map, abs std_def string, defaults "Std": data wells containing standard curve referred . pipetting_direction Can "top_down" (default) "bottom_up". top_down pipetting means curve pipetted vertically (single column 96-well plate), smallest value (blank) row highest value row H. Conversely, bottom_up pipetting blank row H concentrated solution row

title: Extraction and Quality Check (QC) of blank values for standard curves — extract_std_blanc
text: list 4 elements characterizing blank wells: - list$contains supposed blank values (minimum values curve) - list$trusted contains trusted blank values (minimum values wells "correct" row (H)) - list$untrusted contains untrusted wells - list$average contains summarized table per-plate computation average, standard deviation coefficient variation blank values. Note coefficient variation little value computed 2 values, especially values small numbers (typically, blank absorbances often lower 0.1)

title: Extraction and Quality Check (QC) of blank values for standard curves — extract_std_blanc
text: extract_std_blanc() works steps: - First, data corresponding wells containing standard curve extracted (defined parameter std_def) - Second, within "standard data", dataset, plate column (= 1 curve), smallest absorbance value extracted. - check smallest per-curve value indeed found plate row "" (top_down pipetting) row "H" (bottom_up pipetting). - case, wells considered "untrusted" removed "trusted" blank values. - Per-plate averages blank values computed

title: Extraction and Quality Check (QC) of blank values for standard curves — extract_std_blanc
code: # reconstruct a proper data table to start from map_file <- system.file("extdata", "csv_map.csv", package = "plate2N") abs_folder <- system.file("extdata", "txt_examples/", package = "plate2N") map_tibble <- csv_to_tibble(map_file) abs_tibble <- txt_to_tibble(abs_folder) joined_vertical <- join_abs_map(abs_tibble, map_tibble, dataset = "Nmin-") tidy_data <- vertical_to_tidy(joined_vertical)  # run the function list <- tidy_data |> extract_std_blanc(std_def = "Std")

what: Merging 2 vertical plates into one — join_abs_map
title: Merging 2 vertical plates into one — join_abs_map
text: function join_abs_map() thought merge absorbance data mapping counterparts, coming 2 separate import occurrences, single, vertical tibble. takes profit dplyr::left_join() function, connected verticalize_plates() function, provides 2--1 feature verticalizing plates joining .

title: Merging 2 vertical plates into one — join_abs_map
code: join_abs_map(   abs_tibble,   map_tibble,   dataset = "",   abs_map = c("abs-", "map-"),   coerce_numeric = c(FALSE, FALSE) )

title: Merging 2 vertical plates into one — join_abs_map
text: abs_tibble, map_tibble first second tibble (appear left right, respectively). Must structure tibble_example. dataset optional string added prefix column names (tibbles), exception first 2 columns describing well id ("row" "column"). originally meant record name dataset later uses. abs_map string vector add additional prefixes. default value set c("abs", "map"), "abs" data (corresponding argument abs_tibble) receive first prefix, "map" data (corresponding argument map_tibble) receive second prefix. Set c("", "") prevent prefix addition. coerce_numeric logical vector decide whether function verticalize_plates(), called separately abs_tibble map_tibble, coerce data become numeric . default value set c(FALSE, FALSE), eventually data can pivotted single column (see later steps pipeline).

title: Merging 2 vertical plates into one — join_abs_map
text: unique verticalized table containing data data sets.

title: Merging 2 vertical plates into one — join_abs_map
text: first purpose function join absorbance tibble mapping tibble, default setup organized. Still, offers enough flexibility parameters adapted joining 2 tibbles, long fit proper tibble_example-like structure.

title: Merging 2 vertical plates into one — join_abs_map
Example of plate metadata — metadata
text: Must-columns detailed .

title: Example of plate metadata — metadata
Example of plate metadata — metadata
text: tibble 1 row per plate, indefinite number columns dataset,plate_id must plate data (dataset manually encoded import-tidy pipeline) std_sp, std_unit, std_conc characterize standard curve. format std_conc, .digit separator -value separator important. Concentration values MUST ascending order

title: Example of plate metadata — metadata
text: Additional recommended columns recording detail extractant. information can easily get lost, especially relevant variations within lab (ex., K2SO4 vs KCl, extractant concentration, ...). , propose record extractant species (K2SO4), extractant unit (M) extractant concentration (0.5). experimental parameters relevant well (e.g., dilution, incubation time, sampling time, researcher, machine used, wavelength,...) Recording date time can help distinguish batch-effects

title: Multiple distribution plot to review suspicious extractant values and spot outliers — multiplot_outlier_extr

title: Multiple distribution plot to review suspicious extractant values and spot outliers — multiplot_outlier_extr
code: data <- tidy_plates # 0.5 is unreasonable in most uses, but is used here to ensure some output suspicious_plate_id <- qc_raw_extr(data, max_coeff = 0.5,     suppress_message = TRUE, suppress_warning = TRUE) suspicious_extr <- suspicious_extr(data, max_coeff = 0.5,     suspicious_plate_id = suspicious_plate_id) multiplot_outlier_extr(suspicious_extractant = suspicious_extr, max_coeff = 0.5) #> `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value `binwidth`. #> `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value `binwidth`. #> `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value `binwidth`. #> `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value `binwidth`. #> `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value `binwidth`.  ## Tip: if too many curves appear, consider cutting `suspicious_extractant` ##.     into several subsets, to be given as input for multiple runs of `multiplot_outlier_extr()`

what: Display one or more standard curve(s) — plot_std
title: Display one or more standard curve(s) — plot_std
text: Plots raw absorbance (y-axis) vs concentration (x-axis), grouping data plate_id. Uses ggplot2 package.

title: Display one or more standard curve(s) — plot_std
Display one or more standard curve(s) — plot_std
text: std_data table containing data plotted. Must contain columns std_conc, abs, plate_id well_id. plot shows many curves, consider filtering input data frame adding ggplot layer facet (see ?facet_wrap() ?facet_grid()).

title: Display one or more standard curve(s) — plot_std
text: plot one several standard curves.

title: Display one or more standard curve(s) — plot_std
code: data <- tidy_plates |> dplyr::left_join(metadata, by = dplyr::join_by(plate_id)) curve_concentration <- extract_curve(metadata) std_data <- data |>   extract_std_data() |>   dplyr::select(!std_conc) |>   dplyr::left_join(curve_concentration, by = dplyr::join_by(row, plate_id)) plot_std(std_data) + ggplot2::facet_wrap(~plate_id)

what: Quality Check (QC) of raw absorbance data — qc_raw_abs
title: Quality Check (QC) of raw absorbance data — qc_raw_abs
text: qc_raw_abs() extracts data relating wells absorbance outside user-defined range. Whereas long-lasting tradition setting "acceptable" absorbance values 0.1 1 (default values function), reality, acceptable range depend experiment, usage quality spectrophotomer. Note values lower 0.1 rare, especially blank lower values standard curve.

title: Quality Check (QC) of raw absorbance data — qc_raw_abs
code: qc_raw_abs(   data,   min_abs = 0.1,   max_abs = 1,   empty_wells = "empty",   show_msg = TRUE,   show_warning = TRUE,   show_plot = TRUE,   plot_binwidth = 0.01,   plot_col_facet = "dataset",   export_plot = NULL )

title: Quality Check (QC) of raw absorbance data — qc_raw_abs
text: data Tibble, similar form tidy_table. particular, columns map, abs, dataset, plate_id, well_id must present named . min_abs, max_abs Lowest highest accepted value absorbance, default 0.1 1, respectively. empty_wells Character string corresponding empty wells described plate mapping. Defaults "empty", can also vector containing several values. Note wells described NA map column also ignored. show_msg, show_warning Whether keep (TRUE) suppress (FALSE) message warning returned case absence (message) presence (warning) suspicious, --range, wells show_plot Whether display associated plot (histogram absorbance values). Default TRUE. Note next arguments relevant show_plot = TRUE. plot_binwidth set binwidth histogram. Default 0.01 plot_col_facet column use facet plots. facetting, choose plot_col_facet = "none" (now, facet 1 axis possible) export_plot Defaults null. Set string save plot global environment, string name object (e.g., export_plot = "abs_distrib" save object called abs_distrib)

title: Quality Check (QC) of raw absorbance data — qc_raw_abs
text: table 5 columns. first 3 (dataset, plate_id, well_id) allow unique identification suspicious wells can used run remove_wells(). next 2 columns (map, abs) allow visualization suspicious wells

title: Quality Check (QC) of raw absorbance data — qc_raw_abs
code: # bring some NA (abs) and empty wells in tidy_table to check that those wells are removed data <- tidy_table data$abs[1] <- NA data$map[2] <- "empty" qc_raw_abs(data) #> Warning: 957 wells out of 958 are out of range for absorbance, i.e., not in the set boundaries of [0.1; 1].  #> See table to identify suspicious wells.   #> # A tibble: 957 × 5 #>    dataset plate_id well_id map     abs    #>    <chr>   <chr>    <chr>   <chr>   <chr>  #>  1 expe1   M17      A1      Std0193 1.5611 #>  2 expe1   M18      A1      Std0289 1.7013 #>  3 expe1   M19      A1      Std0385 1.6865 #>  4 expe1   M20      A1      Std0481 1.7936 #>  5 expe1   M21      A1      Std0577 1.7925 #>  6 expe1   M22      A1      Std0673 1.8274 #>  7 expe1   M23      A1      Std0769 1.9330 #>  8 expe1   M12      A2      Std0002 1.4802 #>  9 expe1   M16      A2      Std0098 1.5590 #> 10 expe1   M17      A2      Std0194 1.5946 #> # ℹ 947 more rows

text: tibble 960 rows (1 per well 5x2 data plates) 8 columns row 1-letter identifier original plate-row (letters H) column 1-number identifier original plate-column (numbers 1 12) well_id concatenation row column (values A1 H12). well_id repeated 10 times (per data plate) plate_id Unique identifier physical 96-well plate (thus repeated 2x , corresponding absorbance mapping data) unique_well_id concatenation well_id plate_id, identifies uniquely every single well dataset , data comes single dataset called "expe1", information dataset added merging stage, allow filtering tidy_table based dataset map mapping data 96-well plates. can contain sample identifier /treatment identifier. reflects strictly data imported raw data files abs absorbance data 96-well plates. , , corresponds strictly imported data. Note absorbance data still stored character string. Use .double() .numeric() turn numbers

Imports 96-well plate data from .TXT format as exported from the plate reader — txt_to_tibble
text: filepath path folder containing .TXT files. folder may contain non-TXT files, .TXT files within folder included. Warning: "." allowed filename one TXT extension extension default ".TXT". parameter defines pattern files included picked folder identified 'filepath'. output Desired output format. Default tibble. Alternative option list (one element per plate)

title: Imports 96-well plate data from .TXT format as exported from the plate reader — txt_to_tibble
text: Depends output parameter. output = "tibble", tibble plate stacked top (format skanit_to_plate()) output = "list", list element tibble corresponding raw plate data (1 file –> 1 plate –> 1 element). Names elements correspond names files (without extension .TXT). good practice thus name files "plate_name.TXT"

title: From vertical plate data to tidy data using the tidyr package — vertical_to_tidy
code: map_file <- system.file("extdata", "csv_map.csv", package = "plate2N") abs_folder <- system.file("extdata", "txt_examples/", package = "plate2N") map_tibble <- csv_to_tibble(map_file) abs_tibble <- txt_to_tibble(abs_folder) joined_vertical <- join_abs_map(abs_tibble, map_tibble, dataset = "Nmin-") tidy_data <- vertical_to_tidy(joined_vertical)

title: Tidying plate data (verticalization) — verticalize_plates
text: verticalize_plates brings plate data (absorbance mapping data) vertical, tidy format. starts tibble plate format (rendered txt_to_tibble(), csv_to_tibble() skanit_to_tibble()), ends tidy tibble 96 rows (row per well) one column per plate, well structural columns allowing well identification (row, column)

title: Tidying plate data (verticalization) — verticalize_plates
Tidying plate data (verticalization) — verticalize_plates
text: tibble tibble containing raw plate-formatted data tidied. tibble must fit following criteria: - Plate ids single capital letter (e.g., "", "B", ...) - Plates must complete (96 wells accounted , set 12 columns x 8 rows), may contain NA's - Plate data tibble must structured exactly example files (see also txt_to_tibble(), csv_to_tibble() skanit_to_tibble()). coerce_numeric Whether force data entries numerical. default set FALSE, data outputted strings prefix Defaults empty string. prefix can added column names, can useful join tables distinct datasets

title: Tidying plate data (verticalization) — verticalize_plates
text: tidy tibble (verticalized plate data), 1 column per plate
